title: erythroxylum firmum
intro: Erythroxylum firmum, also known as the "Coca of the Andes" or "Coca Bush", is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America, belonging to the Erythroxylum genus of the family Erythroxylaceae. The medicinal properties of Erythroxylum firmum are attributed to its alkaloids, particularly cocaine and tropacocaine, which have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and local anesthetic effects. The health benefits associated with the plant include the reduction of pain and inflammation, as well as the improvement of respiratory and cardiovascular functions. Erythroxylum firmum is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or infused in hot water to be consumed orally, with some cultures also using it as a topical application for wound healing and skin conditions.
